text: Handling deadlines is an essential skill in both study and work. Good time management helps you plan your tasks and avoid last-minute stress. Start by breaking large projects into smaller steps and setting mini-deadlines for each. Prioritization is also key: decide which tasks are most urgent or important, and focus on those first. Sometimes you may need to manage your scope—adjusting or simplifying less critical parts to stay on track. Finally, don’t forget communication. If you foresee a problem, let your team or manager know early. This allows everyone to adjust expectations or offer help.
